homepage.heroSubline: Honest repairs for Wisconsin's harsh winters. Serving Wauwatosa and Brookfield since 2012.
aboutText: Founded in 2012 by lifelong Milwaukee resident Jake Reynolds, Summit Auto Body grew from a one-bay shop to a 20+ team serving the metro area. We understand how Wisconsin winters punish vehicles.
We believe in transparent pricing and thorough explanations. No hidden fees, no upsells—just the right repair for your situation.
From sponsoring Bay View car shows to volunteering at winter driving safety clinics, we're invested in this community.

valueProps.title: Winter-ready repairs
valueProps.description: We use corrosion-resistant materials and techniques proven in Milwaukee's salty winters.

valueProps.title: Local parts network
valueProps.description: Priority access to Milwaukee-area suppliers means faster turnaround on common repairs.

valueProps.title: Insurance specialists
valueProps.description: Our team handles all major Wisconsin insurers to streamline claims.

valueProps.title: Neighborhood knowledge
valueProps.description: We know the parking challenges in the Third Ward and potholes on Bluemound Road.

homepage.ctaSubline: Same-day estimates for Wauwatosa and Brookfield residents.

services.name: Collision Repair
services.shortDescription: Complete restoration after accidents, from fender benders to major impacts.
services.description:
Milwaukee's mix of tight city streets and highway commutes means collision damage is common. We repair everything from minor door dings on Third Ward parking jobs to deer strike damage common on Highway 45.

Our frame straightening equipment handles Wisconsin's worst winter crashes. We match factory specs for European imports common in Brookfield and domestic vehicles alike. Every repair includes corrosion protection to combat road salt damage.
process: Step 1: Free damage assessment with photos and repair options
Step 2: Disassembly and hidden damage inspection
Step 3: Frame correction (if needed) using laser measurement
Step 4: Paint matching and reassembly

faq.question: How long do collision repairs take?
faq.answer: Most repairs take 3-7 days depending on parts availability. We prioritize Milwaukee-area suppliers for faster turnaround.

faq.question: Will my insurance cover this?
faq.answer: We work directly with all major Wisconsin insurers. Our team handles claims paperwork and negotiations.

services.name: Paintless Dent Repair
services.shortDescription: Affordable hail damage and dent removal without repainting.
services.description:
Lake-effect storms dump golf ball-sized hail on Milwaukee cars several times each summer. Our PDR technicians can remove most dents while preserving your factory finish—critical for newer vehicles common in Brookfield's upscale neighborhoods.

We specialize in door dings from tight Bay View parking and shopping cart dents common at Bayshore Mall. The process takes hours instead of days, with no color matching needed. Most repairs cost 50-70% less than conventional methods.
process: Step 1: Dent evaluation under specialized lighting
Step 2: Precision tool access behind panels
Step 3: Gradual reshaping of metal
Step 4: Final polish to restore gloss

faq.question: Can all dents be repaired this way?
faq.answer: PDR works best on shallow dents with intact paint. We'll advise honestly if conventional repair is needed.

faq.question: Does it work on aluminum panels?
faq.answer: Yes, we have specialized tools for newer vehicles with aluminum hoods and doors.

services.name: Rust Repair
services.shortDescription: Stop Wisconsin salt damage before it spreads.
services.description:
Milwaukee's winter road treatment causes severe corrosion—especially on older vehicles common in Wauwatosa's historic districts. We cut out rust completely rather than covering it up, welding in new metal with proper sealing.

Common repairs include rocker panels on trucks used for plowing, wheel arches on SUVs, and trunk floors where snow accumulates. Our anti-corrosion coatings outperform dealer treatments. We also repair salt-damaged brake lines and exhaust systems.
process: Step 1: Rust assessment with scope to check hidden areas
Step 2: Complete removal of affected metal
Step 3: Welding in replacement panels
Step 4: Three-stage corrosion protection

faq.question: How long will rust repairs last?
faq.answer: Properly done repairs typically last 5+ years. We warranty all work against recurrence.

faq.question: Can you match my car's unusual color?
faq.answer: Our paint system matches 98% of factory colors, including vintage shades on classic cars.

services.name: Frame Straightening
services.shortDescription: Precision correction for Milwaukee's worst crashes.
services.description:
When winter black ice sends vehicles into curbs or guardrails, proper frame alignment is critical. Our Milwaukee shop uses computerized measuring systems accurate to 1mm—essential for modern unibody vehicles.

We specialize in repairing Wisconsin's most common collision points: front-end damage from rear-end chain reactions on I-94, and side impacts from intersection slides. Proper alignment ensures your suspension wears evenly and safety systems function correctly.
process: Step 1: Pre-repair measurement with 21-point inspection
Step 2: Gradual hydraulic correction
Step 3: Continuous measurement during pulling
Step 4: Post-repair alignment verification

faq.question: How do I know if my frame is bent?
faq.answer: Signs include doors that won't close properly, uneven tire wear, or steering that pulls after a crash.

faq.question: Is frame damage repairable?
faq.answer: Most modern unibody vehicles can be safely straightened if the damage is within manufacturer tolerances.

services.name: Bumper Repair
services.description:
Milwaukee's tight parallel parking and crowded lots lead to bumper damage year-round. We repair all types from plastic covers on newer imports to chrome bumpers on classic cars.

Our techniques include heat welding for plastic fractures common in cold weather, and rechroming services for vintage vehicles. We match factory textures and finishes—important for luxury vehicles in Brookfield. Most repairs cost 30-50% less than full bumper replacement.
services.shortDescription: Affordable fixes for parking lot scrapes and winter bumps.
process: Step 1: Damage assessment for repair vs replacement
Step 2: Plastic welding or metal straightening
Step 3: Texture and finish matching
Step 4: Reinforced backing for durability

faq.question: Can you repair cracked bumpers?
faq.answer: Yes, we use industrial plastic welders for fractures up to 12 inches long.

faq.question: Do you work with parking sensors?
faq.answer: We recalibrate all factory safety systems after bumper repairs.

reviews.name: Mark T.
reviews.text: Summit fixed the salt damage on my truck's wheel wells perfectly. They even showed me where to apply extra wax to prevent future issues. The color match is spot-on—you'd never know there was work done.

reviews.name: Lisa H.
reviews.text: After the July hailstorm dented my Prius all over, Summit's PDR tech had it looking new again in one day. Saved me from using my insurance deductible. They pointed out two tiny dents I hadn't even noticed.

reviews.name: Carlos R.
reviews.text: When a plow truck slid into my parked Accord in Bay View, Summit handled everything—including dealing with the driver's insurance. The repair looks factory fresh, and they detailed the whole car afterward.

reviews.name: Sarah K.
reviews.text: Fixed my scratched BMW bumper after a Third Ward parking mishap. The texture match is perfect, and they had it done in 24 hours. Cheaper than my insurance deductible would've been.

reviews.name: James P.
reviews.text: Hit a curb on Bluemound Road during an ice storm. Summit's frame machine and alignment check got my Subaru driving straight again. They explained every step clearly.

reviews.name: Aisha B.
reviews.text: Removed a door ding from the Bayshore parking lot without any paintwork. Can't even tell where it was. The technician showed me exactly how he did it—no pressure to upsell anything.

service-areas.title: Auto Body Service Areas Near Milwaukee
service-areas.description: Serving Wauwatosa, Brookfield, and surrounding areas with premium auto-body repairs and rust protection.

serviceAreas.content: Summit Auto Body proudly serves the residents of Wauwatosa, WI, providing top-quality auto-body repairs and collision services tailored to the unique needs of this historic community. With its charming 1920s-40s Tudor and Colonial homes, Wauwatosa's streets are often narrow and winding, making vehicles susceptible to dings and scrapes. Our team is well-versed in handling these common issues, ensuring your car looks as pristine as the neighborhood it calls home. Harsh winters with heavy lake-effect snow can also take a toll on your vehicle's exterior and undercarriage. Trust Summit Auto Body to restore your car after winter weather damage, using durable materials and finishes designed to withstand Milwaukee's toughest seasons.
serviceAreas.localContext: Wauwatosa is known for its historic architecture and tight-knit community, with tree-lined streets and a mix of residential and commercial areas.

serviceAreas.content: Summit Auto Body is your trusted auto-repair partner in Brookfield, WI, where upscale suburban homes from the 1970s-80s reflect the community's pride in maintaining quality. Brookfield's well-maintained vehicles are a testament to its residents' high standards, and we match those expectations with our precision auto-body services. Whether it's a minor scratch from navigating the area's spacious driveways or more significant collision damage, our skilled technicians use advanced techniques to restore your vehicle to like-new condition. The harsh winters here bring heavy lake-effect snow, which can lead to road salt corrosion and winter-related damage. Our rust prevention and repair services ensure your car stays protected year-round.
serviceAreas.localContext: Brookfield is an affluent suburb with a focus on quality living, featuring upscale shopping centers and well-kept residential neighborhoods.

serviceAreas.content: Summit Auto Body extends its expert auto-repair services to West Allis, WI, a bustling city with a mix of industrial and residential areas. Known for its strong working-class roots, West Allis residents rely on their vehicles for daily commutes and errands, making dependable auto-body care essential. Our team specializes in collision repairs, dent removal, and paint matching to keep your car looking its best, whether you're navigating the city's busy streets or parked near its historic homes. Winter weather here can be brutal, with heavy snow and ice causing damage to bumpers and panels. We offer comprehensive winter damage repairs to get your vehicle back on the road safely.
serviceAreas.localContext: West Allis combines a vibrant industrial history with a tight-knit community, featuring local events like the Wisconsin State Fair.

serviceAreas.content: Summit Auto Body is proud to serve Greenfield, WI, a family-friendly suburb with a mix of mid-century and modern homes. The harsh winters in Greenfield bring heavy snowfall and icy roads, leading to potential damage from sliding or salted surfaces.
